subject: Mathematics
title: The Sieve of Eratosthenes
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Sieve of Eratosthenes.

hook.text: An ancient Greek librarian found the fastest way to list every prime number.

buildup.text: Write numbers in a grid. Cross out multiples of 2, then 3, then 5, and so on.

discovery.text: The numbers left standing are all primes. The method works like a filter—hence the name sieve.

twist.text: This method is over 2,200 years old and still taught in computer science classes.

climax.text: Modern variations of the sieve can find primes up to a trillion in seconds.

punchline.text: The oldest algorithm in math still works perfectly.

quiz.question: What does the Sieve of Eratosthenes do?
options: Filters out composite numbers to reveal primes
